\appendix

# Feature Inventory

The tables below catalogue every feature type implemented in the feature
registry (`src/trading_rl/features/`). The active subset used in the main
state-space experiment is marked with a bullet (\textbullet) in the
**Active** column; this subset is defined in
`src/configs/features/hft_lob_features_state_space.yaml`.
Feature names match the keys produced by the feature pipeline at runtime.
Formulas for the active LOB features are given in Chapter 4.

All features used in the work are z-score normalized using training-split
statistics ($\tilde{x}_i = (x_i - \mu_i^{\text{train}}) / \sigma_i^{\text{train}}$)
with two exceptions noted below the tables: the cyclical time encodings, which
are inherently bounded in $[-1, 1]$, and the runtime position feature, which
is already bounded in $[-1, 1]$ by the action space definition.

## LOB Microstructure Features

```{=latex}
\begingroup
\footnotesize
\setlength{\tabcolsep}{3pt}
\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.15}
\begin{longtable}{p{4.6cm}p{2.0cm}p{1.4cm}p{1.1cm}p{4.4cm}}
\toprule
\textbf{Feature name} & \textbf{Group} & \textbf{Param} & \textbf{Active} & \textbf{Literature} \\
\midrule
\endfirsthead
\multicolumn{5}{c}{\textit{(continued from previous page)}} \\
\toprule
\textbf{Feature name} & \textbf{Group} & \textbf{Param} & \textbf{Active} & \textbf{Literature} \\
\midrule
\endhead
\midrule
\multicolumn{5}{r}{\textit{(continued on next page)}} \\
\endfoot
\bottomrule
\endlastfoot
\texttt{hft\_book\_pressure\_l0}       & Imbalance      & level 0   & \textbullet & Cao et al.\ (2004); Biais et al.\ (1995) \\
\texttt{hft\_book\_pressure\_l1}       & Imbalance      & level 1   & \textbullet & Cao et al.\ (2004) \\
\texttt{hft\_book\_pressure\_l2}       & Imbalance      & level 2   & \textbullet & Cao et al.\ (2004) \\
\texttt{hft\_order\_book\_imbalance\_3l} & Imbalance    & $N=3$     & \textbullet & Cont et al.\ (2014) \\
\texttt{hft\_order\_count\_imbalance\_l0} & Imbalance   & level 0   & \textbullet & Biais et al.\ (1995) \\
\texttt{hft\_order\_count\_imbalance\_l1} & Imbalance   & level 1   &            & Biais et al.\ (1995) \\
\texttt{hft\_microprice}               & Fair value     & ---       & \textbullet & Stoikov (2018) \\
\texttt{hft\_microprice\_divergence}   & Fair value     & ---       & \textbullet & Stoikov (2018) \\
\texttt{hft\_vwmp\_skew}               & Fair value     & $N=3$     & \textbullet & Stoikov (2018) \\
\texttt{hft\_price\_vamp\_1k}          & Fair value     & \$1k      &            & Volume-adjusted mid price \\
\texttt{hft\_price\_vamp\_5k}          & Fair value     & \$5k      &            & Volume-adjusted mid price \\
\texttt{hft\_depth\_ratio}             & Depth          & $N=4$     &            & Bouchaud et al.\ (2002) \\
\texttt{hft\_spread\_bps}              & Spread/cost    & ---       & \textbullet & Kyle (1985); Glosten \& Milgrom (1985) \\
\texttt{hft\_spread\_ratio\_50}        & Spread/cost    & $W=50$    & \textbullet & Easley et al.\ (2012) \\
\texttt{hft\_spread\_ratio\_200}       & Spread/cost    & $W=200$   &            & Easley et al.\ (2012) \\
\texttt{hft\_bid\_convexity}           & Spread/cost    & bid side  & \textbullet & Bouchaud et al.\ (2002) \\
\texttt{hft\_ask\_convexity}           & Spread/cost    & ask side  & \textbullet & Bouchaud et al.\ (2002) \\
\texttt{hft\_bid\_slope}               & Spread/cost    & 5 levels  & \textbullet & Bouchaud et al.\ (2002); Kyle (1985) \\
\texttt{hft\_ask\_slope}               & Spread/cost    & 5 levels  & \textbullet & Bouchaud et al.\ (2002); Kyle (1985) \\
\texttt{hft\_ofi}                      & Order flow     & ---       & \textbullet & Cont et al.\ (2014) \\
\texttt{hft\_ofi\_rolling\_10}         & Order flow     & $W=10$    &            & Cont et al.\ (2014) \\
\texttt{hft\_ofi\_rolling\_50}         & Order flow     & $W=50$    & \textbullet & Cont et al.\ (2014) \\
\texttt{hft\_ofi\_rolling\_200}        & Order flow     & $W=200$   &            & Cont et al.\ (2014) \\
\texttt{hft\_ofi\_multilevel\_3l}      & Order flow     & $N=3$     &            & Cont et al.\ (2014) \\
\texttt{hft\_ofi\_multilevel\_5l}      & Order flow     & $N=5$     &            & Cont et al.\ (2014) \\
\texttt{hft\_bid\_queue\_depletion}    & Order flow     & bid side  & \textbullet & Foucault et al.\ (2005) \\
\texttt{hft\_ask\_queue\_depletion}    & Order flow     & ask side  & \textbullet & Foucault et al.\ (2005) \\
\texttt{hft\_signed\_trade\_flow\_50}  & Order flow     & $W=50$    & \textbullet & Lee \& Ready (1991) \\
\texttt{hft\_signed\_trade\_flow\_200} & Order flow     & $W=200$   &            & Lee \& Ready (1991) \\
\texttt{hft\_odd\_lot\_trade\_ratio\_200} & Order flow  & $W=200$   & \textbullet & Boehmer et al.\ (2021) \\
\texttt{hft\_odd\_lot\_imbalance\_200} & Order flow     & $W=200$   & \textbullet & Boehmer et al.\ (2021) \\
\texttt{hft\_vpin\_100}                & Order flow     & $W=100$   &            & Easley et al.\ (2012) \\
\texttt{hft\_vpin\_500}                & Order flow     & $W=500$   &            & Easley et al.\ (2012) \\
\texttt{hft\_cancel\_to\_trade\_200}   & Order flow     & $W=200$   &            & Foucault et al.\ (2005) \\
\texttt{hft\_cancel\_to\_trade\_1000}  & Order flow     & $W=1000$  &            & Foucault et al.\ (2005) \\
\texttt{hft\_trade\_arrival\_rate\_100} & Order flow    & $W=100$   &            & Engle (2000) \\
\texttt{hft\_trade\_arrival\_rate\_500} & Order flow    & $W=500$   &            & Engle (2000) \\
\texttt{hft\_large\_trade\_ratio\_200} & Order flow     & $W=200$   &            & Institutional flow proxy \\
\texttt{hft\_ofi\_autocorr\_50}        & Order flow     & $W=50$    &            & Cont et al.\ (2014) \\
\texttt{hft\_ofi\_autocorr\_200}       & Order flow     & $W=200$   &            & Cont et al.\ (2014) \\
\texttt{hft\_inter\_event\_time}       & Regime/temp.   & ---       & \textbullet & Engle (2000) \\
\texttt{hft\_mid\_price\_acceleration} & Regime/temp.   & ---       & \textbullet & Abergel et al.\ (2016) \\
\texttt{hft\_hour\_sin}                & Regime/temp.   & ---       & \textbullet & Wood et al.\ (1985) \\
\texttt{hft\_hour\_cos}                & Regime/temp.   & ---       & \textbullet & Wood et al.\ (1985) \\
\end{longtable}
\endgroup
```

## Bar-Level and Technical Features

The features below operate on OHLCV bar data and are not used in the main
HFT experiment but are available in the feature registry for medium-frequency
trading (MFT) scenarios and ablation studies.

```{=latex}
\begingroup
\footnotesize
\setlength{\tabcolsep}{3pt}
\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.15}
\begin{longtable}{p{4.0cm}p{2.4cm}p{2.0cm}p{4.0cm}}
\toprule
\textbf{Feature name} & \textbf{Group} & \textbf{Param} & \textbf{Notes} \\
\midrule
\endfirsthead
\multicolumn{4}{c}{\textit{(continued from previous page)}} \\
\toprule
\textbf{Feature name} & \textbf{Group} & \textbf{Param} & \textbf{Notes} \\
\midrule
\endhead
\midrule
\multicolumn{4}{r}{\textit{(continued on next page)}} \\
\endfoot
\bottomrule
\endlastfoot
\texttt{log\_return}          & Price      & ---             & Log return of close \\
\texttt{high}                & Price      & ---             & High / close ratio \\
\texttt{low}                 & Price      & ---             & Low / close ratio \\
\texttt{simple\_return}      & Price      & ---             & Simple return \\
\texttt{trend}               & Price      & ---             & Not normalized \\
\texttt{rsi}                 & Price      & $P=14$          & Relative strength index \\
\texttt{return\_lag\_1}      & Price      & lag 1           & Lagged close return \\
\texttt{log\_volume}         & Volume     & ---             & Log of volume \\
\texttt{volume\_change}      & Volume     & ---             & Volume change rate \\
\texttt{volume\_ma\_ratio\_20} & Volume   & $W=20$          & Volume / MA ratio \\
\texttt{amihud\_illiquidity} & Volume     & $W=1$           & Amihud (2002) \\
\texttt{relative\_volume}   & Volume     & ---             & Relative volume ratio \\
\texttt{realized\_volatility\_20} & Volatility & $W=20$     & Realized vol \\
\texttt{volatility\_ratio\_5\_60} & Volatility & $S{=}5, L{=}60$ & Short / long vol ratio \\
\texttt{hour\_sin}           & Temporal   & ---             & Not normalized$^a$ \\
\texttt{hour\_cos}           & Temporal   & ---             & Not normalized$^a$ \\
\texttt{day\_of\_week\_sin}  & Temporal   & ---             & Not normalized$^a$ \\
\texttt{day\_of\_week\_cos}  & Temporal   & ---             & Not normalized$^a$ \\
\texttt{minute\_of\_hour\_sin} & Temporal & ---             & Not normalized$^a$ \\
\texttt{minute\_of\_hour\_cos} & Temporal & ---             & Not normalized$^a$ \\
\texttt{sma\_20}             & TA-Lib trend     & $P=20$    & Simple moving average \\
\texttt{ema\_20}             & TA-Lib trend     & $P=20$    & Exponential moving average \\
\texttt{wma\_20}             & TA-Lib trend     & $P=20$    & Weighted moving average \\
\texttt{dema\_20}            & TA-Lib trend     & $P=20$    & Double EMA \\
\texttt{tema\_20}            & TA-Lib trend     & $P=20$    & Triple EMA \\
\texttt{talib\_rsi\_14}      & TA-Lib momentum  & $P=14$    & RSI (TA-Lib) \\
\texttt{macd\_12\_26\_9}     & TA-Lib momentum  & $f{=}12, s{=}26, g{=}9$ & MACD \\
\texttt{mom\_10}             & TA-Lib momentum  & $P=10$    & Momentum \\
\texttt{roc\_10}             & TA-Lib momentum  & $P=10$    & Rate of change \\
\texttt{cmo\_14}             & TA-Lib momentum  & $P=14$    & Chande momentum oscillator \\
\texttt{willr\_14}           & TA-Lib momentum  & $P=14$    & Williams \%R \\
\texttt{cci\_14}             & TA-Lib momentum  & $P=14$    & Commodity channel index \\
\texttt{atr\_14}             & TA-Lib volatility & $P=14$   & Average true range \\
\texttt{natr\_14}            & TA-Lib volatility & $P=14$   & Normalized ATR \\
\texttt{bbands\_20}          & TA-Lib volatility & $P=20$   & Bollinger bands \\
\texttt{obv}                 & TA-Lib volume    & ---       & On-balance volume \\
\texttt{ad}                  & TA-Lib volume    & ---       & Accumulation / distribution \\
\texttt{adosc\_3\_10}        & TA-Lib volume    & $f{=}3, s{=}10$ & AD oscillator \\
\texttt{adx\_14}             & TA-Lib trend str.& $P=14$    & Average directional index \\
\texttt{aroon\_14}           & TA-Lib trend str.& $P=14$    & Aroon oscillator \\
\end{longtable}
\endgroup
```

$^a$ Cyclical time encodings are inherently bounded in $[-1, 1]$ and
require no normalization. The runtime position feature
(`feature_position` $\in [-1, 1]$) is likewise not normalized; it is
already bounded by the action space definition. All other features are
z-score normalized on the training split only.

---

# TD3 Hyperparameters

The table below lists all hyperparameters for the main AAPL state-space
experiment. The full configuration file is
`src/configs/scenarios/aapl_td3_hft_lob_state_space.yaml`.

```{=latex}
\begingroup
\footnotesize
\setlength{\tabcolsep}{4pt}
\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.2}
\begin{longtable}{p{4.4cm}p{2.2cm}p{5.6cm}}
\toprule
\textbf{Parameter} & \textbf{Value} & \textbf{Notes} \\
\midrule
\endfirsthead
\multicolumn{3}{c}{\textit{(continued from previous page)}} \\
\toprule
\textbf{Parameter} & \textbf{Value} & \textbf{Notes} \\
\midrule
\endhead
\midrule
\multicolumn{3}{r}{\textit{(continued on next page)}} \\
\endfoot
\bottomrule
\endlastfoot

\textbf{Architecture} & & \\
Actor hidden dims & [128, 64] & ReLU activations \\
Critic hidden dims & [128, 64] & ReLU activations \\

\textbf{Optimization} & & \\
Actor learning rate & 0.0001 & Adam \\
Critic learning rate & 0.0001 & Adam \\
Actor weight decay & 0.000002 & L2 regularization \\
Critic weight decay & 0.000002 & \\
Loss function & Smooth L1 (Huber) & \\

\textbf{TD3 mechanisms} & & \\
Discount factor $\gamma$ & 0.995 & \\
Target update $\tau$ & 0.001 & Soft update \\
Policy delay & 2 & Critic updates per actor update \\
Target policy noise $\sigma$ & 0.2 & Added to target action \\
Noise clip & 0.3 & \\
Exploration noise std & 0.1 & During training only \\

\textbf{Training} & & \\
Total steps & 2,000,000 & \\
Initial random steps & 1,000 & Warm-up before learning \\
Frames per batch & 200 & \\
Optimisation steps per batch & 5 & \\
Mini-batch size & 128 & \\
Replay buffer size & 100,000 & \\

\textbf{Data} & & \\
Training rows & 15,000 & Chronological split \\
Reward type & Differential Sharpe & $\eta = 0.01$ \\
Transaction fee & 0.000 & Baseline setting \\
Random seed & 42 & \\
\end{longtable}
\endgroup
```

---

# Experiment Configuration Example

The YAML snippet below is a condensed version of the main experiment
configuration, illustrating the configuration-driven workflow described in
Chapter 5.

```yaml
seed: 42

data:
  data_path: "./data/raw/stocks/AAPL_2026-02-25_2026-02-27_raw_mbp-10_us_hours.parquet"
  feature_config: "src/configs/features/hft_lob_features_state_space.yaml"
  train_size: 15000

env:
  backend: "tradingenv"
  mode: "hft"
  trading_fees: 0.000
  price_column: "close"
  reward_type: "differential_sharpe"
  reward_eta: 0.01

network:
  actor_hidden_dims: [128, 64]
  value_hidden_dims: [128, 64]

training:
  algorithm: "TD3"
  actor_lr: 0.0001
  value_lr: 0.0001
  gamma: 0.995
  tau: 0.001
  policy_noise: 0.2
  noise_clip: 0.3
  policy_delay: 2
  exploration_noise_std: 0.1
  max_steps: 2000000
  sample_size: 128
  buffer_size: 100000
  loss_function: "smooth_l1"
```

---

# TD3 Training Workflow Diagram

The diagram below summarizes the configuration-driven workflow described in
Chapter 5.

```{=latex}
\begin{center}
```

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-align: center
%%{init: {
  'theme': 'base',
  'themeVariables': {
    'fontSize': '11px',
    'primaryColor': '#f5f4ff',
    'primaryBorderColor': '#a78bfa',
    'lineColor': '#6b7280'
  },
  'flowchart': {
    'nodeSpacing': 18,
    'rankSpacing': 20,
    'padding': 4,
    'useMaxWidth': false
  }
}}%%
flowchart TD
    A[Scenario + feature config] --> B[Pre-run validation]
    B --> C[build_prepared_dataset]
    C --> D[PreparedDataset<br/>train / val / test + metadata]
    D --> E[build_training_bundle]
    E --> F[TrainingBundle<br/>train env + trainer + callback]
    F --> G[Train TD3]
    G --> H[Checkpoint + MLflow log]
    H --> I[evaluate_all_splits]
    I --> J[Resolve primary split<br/>test -> val -> train]
    J --> K[Primary-split explainability]
    K --> L[Metrics + robustness outputs]
```

```{=latex}
\end{center}
```

---

# TD3 Update Mechanics Diagram

The diagram below summarizes the core TD3 update mechanics used in the thesis
implementation, abstracting away from project-specific plumbing.

```{=latex}
\begin{center}
```

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-align: center
%%{init: {
  'theme': 'base',
  'themeVariables': {
    'fontSize': '10px',
    'primaryColor': '#f5f4ff',
    'primaryBorderColor': '#a78bfa',
    'lineColor': '#6b7280'
  },
  'flowchart': {
    'nodeSpacing': 18,
    'rankSpacing': 18,
    'padding': 4,
    'useMaxWidth': false
  }
}}%%
{{< include ./diagrams/td3_update.mmd >}}
```

```{=latex}
\end{center}
```

---

# RL Algorithm Taxonomy

The diagram below maps the reinforcement learning landscape from the Bellman
equation through the key algorithmic relaxations, showing where TD3 sits
relative to other families of methods. Source: author's own diagram, based on
@Sutton2018.

```{=latex}
\begin{center}
```

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-align: center
%%| fig-width: 6.4
%%| fig-height: 8.1
%%{init: {'theme': 'base', 'themeVariables': {'fontSize': '8px'}, 'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 10, 'rankSpacing': 14, 'padding': 3, 'useMaxWidth': true}}}%%
graph TD
    A[MDP] -->|Solution| B(Bellman Equation)
    B --> C{Known model?}
    C -->|Yes| D[Dynamic Programming]
    C -->|No| E[Reinforcement Learning]
    E --> F{Value estimation?}
    F -->|Full episode| G[Monte Carlo]
    F -->|Bootstrap| H[Temporal Difference<br/>SARSA / Q-Learning]
    E --> I{State space?}
    I -->|Small / Discrete| J[Tabular Methods]
    I -->|Large / Continuous| K[Function Approximation<br/>Deep RL]
    K --> L{Action space?}
    L -->|Discrete| M[Value-Based<br/>DQN / Rainbow]
    L -->|Continuous| N[Policy-Based<br/>REINFORCE / PPO]
    L -->|Either| O[Actor-Critic]
    O --> O1{On/Off-Policy?}
    O1 -->|On-Policy| O2[A2C / PPO]
    O1 -->|Off-Policy| O3[DDPG / TD3 / SAC]
    O3 --> O4{Policy type?}
    O4 -->|Deterministic| O5[DDPG]
    O4 -->|Twin critics +<br/>delayed updates| O6[TD3 — This Thesis]
    O4 -->|Max entropy| O7[SAC]
    E --> P{Sample efficiency?}
    P -->|Need planning| Q[Model-Based RL<br/>Dyna-Q / Dreamer]
    P -->|Model-free OK| R[Model-Free RL]
```

```{=latex}
\end{center}
```

---

# TD3 Network Architecture

The diagram below shows how the actor, twin critics, and replay buffer interact
in the TD3 architecture used here.

```{=latex}
\begin{center}
```

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-align: center
%%| fig-width: 5
%%| fig-height: 3.5
%%{init: {'theme': 'base', 'themeVariables': {'fontSize': '9px'}, 'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 12, 'rankSpacing': 16, 'padding': 3, 'useMaxWidth': true}}}%%
graph TD
    S[State s] --> A1[Actor mu_theta]
    A1 --> A2[Action a]
    A2 --> N1[Gaussian Noise added]
    N1 --> A3[Noisy Action]
    S --> C1[Critic Q_phi1]
    S --> C2[Critic Q_phi2]
    A2 --> C1
    A2 --> C2
    C1 --> MIN[min]
    C2 --> MIN
    MIN --> T1[Target Value]
    RB[Replay Buffer<br/>100k transitions] --> C1
    RB --> C2
    RB --> A1
```

```{=latex}
\end{center}
```

---

# Data Preparation Pipeline

The diagram below shows the feature pipeline design that ensures no information
from the validation or test splits leaks into the fitted normalization
parameters.

```{=latex}
\begin{center}
```

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-align: center
%%| fig-width: 3.5
%%| fig-height: 6.8
%%{init: {'theme': 'base', 'themeVariables': {'fontSize': '8px'}, 'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 10, 'rankSpacing': 14, 'padding': 3, 'useMaxWidth': true}}}%%
{{< include ./diagrams/data_pipeline.mmd >}}
```

```{=latex}
\end{center}
```
